name: Log signal overlay on video player
description: Add a signal timeline below the multi-camera video player that stays in sync with playback. Shows speed as a line chart, engagement state as a colored band (engaged = green, disengaged = gray, alert = red), and steering angle as a second line. Clicking anywhere on the timeline seeks the video. Pulls from /v1/routes/:dongleId/:routeName/signals.
acceptance_criteria: web/src/components/video/SignalTimeline.tsx is a new component that fetches the signals endpoint and renders three horizontally-aligned tracks: engaged state band, speed line, steering line
Takes currentTime (seconds, route-relative) and onSeek(seconds) props and draws a vertical playhead that moves with currentTime; clicking the timeline calls onSeek

Integrated into the existing route detail page (web/src/app/routes/[dongleId]/[routeName]/page.tsx) below the multi-camera player; uses the player's onTimeUpdate to feed currentTime and its seek method for onSeek
Rendering is done via a simple <canvas> (no heavy charting lib needed); handles window resize and device pixel ratio
Graceful fallback: if the signals endpoint returns empty arrays, the component shows 'No log data' instead of a blank box
pnpm --dir web lint and pnpm --dir web type-check pass
Manual check: load a route with uploaded qlogs, scrub the video, verify the playhead follows and clicking the timeline seeks
